jobs.description:
Fire TV Channels (FTVC) BD is looking for a talented, self-starting Senior BD to join the global FTVC BD team. This person will have two primary objectives. First, this person will be a Creator & Podcasts expert in the US. This responsibility will involve working with digital agencies, talent managers, and creative talent directly and educating them on the FTVC opportunity. This person will provide a critical feedback loop to the FTVC Product team as it builds tools (e.g. a self-service portal for publishers) and policies (e.g. content moderation and takedown) for Creators & Podcasts. Second, this person will be own our Fire TV Channels Content Investment Program.

Key job responsibilities
- Execute agreements with and onboard over 30 Creators and Podcasts globally.
- Assist with FTVC GTM strategy for Creators and Podcasts.
- Scale our Content Investment Program in partnership with Product/Editorial.

jobs.description:
The Core Business Growth & Ops Finance Partner serves as an embedded finance partner and technical specialist for critical E-Commerce operational workstreams. This role requires deep analytical capabilities combined with the ability to translate complex operational metrics into financial impact. The position supports multiple high-priority areas including Delivery/Pickup, Subscriptions/A la Carte, and Topology/Capacity, requiring versatility and strong prioritization skills.

Key job responsibilities
Serve as embedded finance partner for Delivery and Pickup operations, providing financial analysis and decision support
• Own financial modeling and performance tracking for Subscriptions and ALC (Automated Lockers) initiatives
• Support Topology and Capacity planning with financial analysis, scenario modeling, and investment recommendations
• Develop technical financial models that connect operational drivers to P&L impact
• Partner with operations teams to identify cost optimization and efficiency improvement opportunities
• Provide variance analysis and actionable insights on core business performance metrics
• Build and maintain dashboards that track key operational and financial KPIs
• Support ad-hoc analytical requests requiring deep-dive investigation and technical expertise
jobs.description_short: The Core Business Growth & Ops Finance Partner serves as an embedded finance partner and technical specialist for critical E-Commerce operational workstreams. This role requires deep analytical capabilities

jobs.description:
The Reliability Maintenance Engineering (RME) team at Amazon is fundamental to our operations – they’re the ones keeping vital machinery running at all times. As an RME Technician, you’ll maintain a wide range of equipment and workspaces while upholding Health and Safety best practices. You'll help to keep your colleagues safe and prevent machine downtime. Our RME Technicians boost the availability and quality of our equipment and work to enhance the operational environment too.

Key job responsibilities
• Proactive and preventative maintenance tasks on a wide range of site equipment
• Carry out reactive repairs and fault diagnosis in a live distribution warehouse
• Use the latest tools to maximise equipment effectiveness
• Uphold all health and safety policies and practices
• Support the development and progression of on-site apprentices
• Work on continuous improvement projects and roll out best practices across a range of EU sites

A day in the life
Our RME Technicians are based on-site, so they can respond quickly to any machinery issues. You’ll carry out a range of planned preventative maintenance to our sites’ Make On Demand (MOD) equipment to make sure it’s working safely.

As an RME Technician, you’ll also respond promptly to breakdowns, investigating, finding, and implementing solutions as quickly as possible. You’ll also raise suggestions and implement projects for long-term improvements.

Working alongside senior colleagues, you’ll undertake day-to-day maintenance tasks and liaise with contractors if you need external expertise. You’ll work on a range of shifts to make sure our sites are supported around the clock – allowing Amazon to deliver at the speed we’re known for.

About the team
Our Reliability Maintenance Engineering or RME team keep our equipment performing at its best. We're a technically minded team, made up of excellent team players and guided by experienced leaders. We work together to maintain, troubleshoot and repair equipment across our global network of fulfilment centres. Some of our people are leaders, who oversee the team as they work on exciting technology – some of which only exists at Amazon.

From installing automated systems to overseeing the general maintenance of a site to repairing key distribution equipment, our team handles most aspects of Amazon’s technical side. This includes keeping buildings legally compliant, so everyone is safe, and our sites are operating at maximum efficiency. There are lots of opportunities for progression, both within the RME team and more widely across Amazon.

Everything we do focuses on reducing downtime in Amazon’s crucial operations sites, so customers get their orders on time. We often work during early or late hours to carry out maintenance with minimal disruption, including night shifts. If we spot a better way to do something, we have the skills and the opportunity to develop and introduce completely new processes or innovative technology such as Amazon Robotics and Complex Sorters.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Juris Doctor and membership in one state bar or equivalent
- 2+ years of legal experience
- Knowledge of export control or strategic control laws
- Export Control Requirement: Due to applicable export control laws and regulations, candidates must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (i.e., current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.
jobs.description:
Amazon's Legal Department is looking for a talented export controls lawyer to support Amazon Web Services (AWS). AWS is at the forefront of the cloud computing and service industry providing IT infrastructure services such as on-demand compute capacity, storage, content delivery, database services, artificial intelligence and machine learning, and more.

We are seeking an export controls attorney to join our international trade legal team.

Key job responsibilities
- Provide counsel on export laws and new technology such as AI, quantum, and semiconductors;
- Support regulatory engagements;
- Collaborate globally to develop innovative international trade compliance solutions; and
- Research and stay up to date on forthcoming export control laws and enforcement trends.

A day in the life
The role involves working directly with business units and cross-functional teams across AWS and other parts of Amazon. The successful candidate will provide strategic input on legal and business decisions, navigate complex regulatory environments, and develop problem solving solutions. This position plays a crucial role in supporting AWS globally and requires a talented and motivated individual capable of managing a broad range of matters, in a rapidly evolving business landscape.

About the team
This role will give the successful candidate an opportunity to join a collaborative, tight-knit team that is leading exciting and pioneering global initiatives as part of a business that is making history. Some travel, domestic and international, will be required.
jobs.description_short: Amazon's Legal Department is looking for a talented export controls lawyer to support Amazon Web Services (AWS). AWS is at the forefront of the cloud computing and service industry providing IT infrastructure

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 10+ years of electrical theory, robotics, controls components, and automated equipment experience
- Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering or a related field
- 4+ years of motor controls (motor starts, variable frequency drives, DC drives, and standard electrical components) experience
- Experience developing products for large volume production
- Experience working effectively across cross-functional teams and partnering well with people at all levels within an organization
- Strong PCB design and layout skills (Altium, KiCad, or similar) with an understanding of high-power circuits, signal integrity, and thermal constraints
- Experience with lithium-ion battery systems, BMS hardware/software, and power distribution architectures
- Strong debugging and troubleshooting skills, including experience with oscilloscopes, power analyzers, and motor test benches
- Proficiency with embedded system interfaces (FDCAN, SPI, I2C, RS485, USB) and experience working with STM32 microcontrollers
- Knowledge of EMI/EMC mitigation techniques and compliance standards for industrial or robotics systems
- Experience working in robotics, automotive, aerospace, or other real-time electromechanical systems
jobs.description:
We are looking for a Senior Electrical Engineer to lead the design and development of our power and motion control systems for our robotic systems. This role is an opportunity to lead the long-term architecture of our robotics platform and help build the technical foundation for robots and humans working together.

Key job responsibilities
- Lead the design, layout, and validation of PCBs for power distribution, motor drivers, embedded systems, and sensor integration

- Architect and optimize power distribution and battery system to support a robot with high-performance actuators and real-time compute workloads

- Develop and refine our lithium-ion battery system

- Develop high-efficiency system power architecture and power monitoring circuitry to support our robotics platform

- Ensure EMI/EMC compliance, signal integrity, and thermal design best practices for high-reliability electronics in a compact mobile system

- Collaborate with firmware engineers on real-time control strategies for smooth, compliant motion

- Work with mechanical engineers to integrate thermal management solutions for batteries, high-power electronics, and motors

- Define the electrical architecture for real-time control, networking, and system diagnostics, ensuring high-speed, low-latency communication between actuators, sensors, and compute nodes

- Support bring-up, debugging, and testing of prototype robots, working closely with firmware and mechanical teams

- Work with vendors and contract manufacturers to source high-quality components and oversee the production of key electronics subsystems

- Mentor junior engineers, drive design reviews, and define electrical engineering best practices

About the team
Fauna Robotics, an Amazon company, is building capable, safe, and genuinely delightful robots for everyday life. Our goal is simple: make robots people actually want to live and interact with in everyday human spaces.
We believe that future won’t arrive until building for robotics becomes far more accessible. Today, too much effort is spent reinventing the fundamentals. We’re changing that by developing tightly integrated hardware and software systems that make it faster, safer, and more intuitive to create real-world robotic products.
Our work spans the full stack: mechanical design, control systems, dynamic modeling, and intelligent software. The focus is not just functionality, but experience. We’re building robots that feel responsive, expressive, and genuinely useful.
At Fauna, you’ll work at the frontier of this space, helping define how robots move, manipulate, and interact with people in natural environments. It’s an opportunity to solve hard problems across hardware and software with a team focused on making robotics accessible and joyful to build.
If you care about making robotics real for everyone and building systems that are as delightful as they are capable, we’re interested in hearing from you.
jobs.description_short: We are looking for a Senior Electrical Engineer to lead the design and development of our power and motion control systems for our robotic systems. This role is an opportunity to lead the long-term architecture

jobs.description:
Our Reliability Maintenance Engineering (RME) team is central to Amazon's commitment to innovation. As Amazon evolves and adapts, this team makes sure that the tools and technologies we use do as well. As a Senior RME Technician, you'll help us stay one step ahead, adopting the latest technologies and identifying new and efficient ways of working. In this role, you’ll pay close attention to our processes to help maintain our high standards, and you’ll put in place upgrades to take that standard even higher.

Key job responsibilities
- Demonstrate best practice on using equipment safely and monitor teams to ensure everyone follows the correct processes
- Keep track of equipment performance through visual checks, condition-based monitoring, and preventative maintenance tasks and schedule additional servicing when required
- Supervise technicians on shift to support their development and act as the first point of contact for Reliability Maintenance Engineers
- Solve issues in equipment to reduce downtime for operations so they can process packages as quickly as possible
- Support in finding ways to continually improve systems and standardise processes across the EU network

A day in the life
In every shift, you’ll be leading a team to make sure everyone has the equipment they need and that it’s running properly. That involves monitoring processes so that regulations and policies are followed and ensuring that preventative maintenance is carried out to increase equipment uptime.

With everything you observe on your shifts, you’ll suggest improvements to make our systems more efficient and productive and then implement those improvements across an EU-wide function. You’ll also be monitoring processes with tools like Scada to guarantee their performance, as well as working closely with your line manager to assist with admin tasks.

This role is located at one of our operational sites. You’ll work shift patterns, which may include some weekends and nights. As you develop your experience within this exciting part of Amazon, you’ll be well-placed to progress into more senior roles.

About the team
Our Reliability Maintenance Engineering or RME team keep our equipment performing at its best. We're a technically minded team, made up of excellent team players and guided by experienced leaders. We work together to maintain, troubleshoot and repair equipment across our global network of fulfilment centres. Some of our people are leaders, who oversee the team as they work on exciting technology – some of which only exists at Amazon.

From installing systems to overseeing the general maintenance of a site to repairing key distribution equipment, our team handles most aspects of Amazon’s technical side. This includes keeping buildings legally compliant, so everyone is safe, and our sites are operating at maximum efficiency. There are lots of opportunities for progression, both within the RME team and more widely across Amazon.

Everything we do focuses on reducing downtime in Amazon’s crucial operations sites, so customers get their orders on time. We often work during early or late hours to carry out maintenance with minimal disruption, including night shifts. If we spot a better way to do something, we have the skills and the opportunity to develop and introduce completely new processes or cutting-edge tech.

jobs.description:
At Amazon, we are working to be the most customer-centric company on earth, therefore your role as a manager is key to achieving a balance in the role.

Amazon’s Operations team is seeking a highly skilled and motivated our team on the management of daily inventory control, quality assurance and capacity activities for our Warehouse. Inventory control and quality assurance activities play a critical role in leading best sortation, delivery practices, and capacity management inside the Warehouse and thereby has direct impact on Amazon's ability to serve customers on time.

Key job responsibilities
· Create balance between “Work hard” and “Have fun” with associates under scope
· Internal audits execution, ensure the team execution to accomplish the business goals
· Support process improvement activities associated with process inefficiencies and/or new functions currently not performed in day-to-day operations
· Assist in the development, documentation, deployment and monitoring the key business processes which support all internal processes of AMZL Mexico.
· Follow up and dive deep the KPIs of operations within the delivery station, implement and develop the delivery station’s workflow and metrics according to the standard work and book of processes, to achieve year-over-year improvements.
· Actively supports and promotes organization change.
· Measure drivers’ performance, discipline and customer experience service, processes Mapping activities, implement change and/or develop new processes as required to provide ability to better service Amazon customers, focus on reducing cost.


A day in the life
Support, mentor, and motivate your hourly workforce.

Manage safety, quality, productivity, and customer delivery promises

Collaborate with all support teams including Safety, Engineering, Loss Prevention, Quality Assurance, Human Resources to develop plans to meet business objectives,

Lift up to 49 pounds and frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach

Stand/walk for up to 12 hours during shifts

Work in an environment where the noise level varies and can be loud

Work in an environment that is subject to variable temperatures and weather (delivery stations include outside loading departments)

Continuously climb and descend stairs (applies to sites with stairs)


About the team
Amazon opened its doors in 1995 and strives to be the world’s most customer-centric company, where customers can find and discover anything they might want to buy online.
Delivery Station Manager is the support for the complete mile operations within a delivery station is a key of the last mile operation. The role is responsible for managing a +20 hourly team, safety metrics, attrition, payroll, shifts, vacations, overtime and the AMZL OPS metrics; delivery efficiency, number of routes, cost per shipment. In addition, this role gives support in purchasing tools, services and consumables for the station.

jobs.description:
As a Sr. Product Manager-Technical in WW Ops Finance Standardization & Automation you will lead the vision, roadmap and execution by partnering with other Product Managers, Finance partners, BI Managers, Data Engineers, BIEs, and TPMs. You will understand the inputs from multiple internal and external sources and develop a product vision and strategy to achieve the goals of Ops Finance Standardization and Automation team. You will own data platforms products in S&A space. We have a strong vision and iterating rapidly with incremental value to our customers and this will provide a highly visible charter and strategic significance. This comes with a lot of opportunities to develop/launch a new suite of technical product capabilities to manage our data ecosystem and also grow as a product leader, as well as ambiguity in ownership and direction in early days.

About you
You’re an accountable, hands-on product leader with strong focus on problem solving with a positive attitude, who lets nothing stand in the way. You know how to clarify the path forward and prioritize work while dealing with ambiguity and limited data points. You know how to influence others. You’re a natural collaborator equally comfortable talking business process with operation leaders, tech with developers, user experience with designers, positioning with product marketers, numbers with finance and marketing, and strategy with executive leadership. You stay in course for your vision, but be flexible in how you achieve it keeping overall success and incremental value to your customers in mind.


Key job responsibilities
The critical functions for this leader are as follows:
Own one or more products in Data Platforms:
- Be the ultimate owner of the product vision, strategy and execution for your product area.
- Create and manage the roadmap, prioritization frameworks, entitlement and success metrics
- Recruit customers to be part of advisors, share roadmap with customer stakeholders and partners
- Designing and implementing new processes to delight Finance customers at scale.
- Implement and manage a Product/Program Management framework that ensures completeness of requirements and communication with stakeholders through scalable mechanisms such as business reviews, news flashes, dashboards.


A day in the life
As a Product Leader in S&A, you are empowered to make data-driven decisions, articulate your vision and sell your features for implementation. You are expected to articulate your point of view with crisp communication, demonstrate ability to synthesize the inputs from your partners and stakeholders, and achieve alignment in harmonious way. You will proactively manage product backlog and work closely with your tech team and TPMs to ensure deliveries that deliver both value and take the product towards S&A North Star vision.

About the team
WW Ops Finance S&A Data Platforms team is tasked to come up with data strategy and implement it with scalable platforms and products. These products support automation and standardization of finance routines such as planning and reporting. We build features and products that span the data stack using Medallion architecture.

jobs.description:
Amazon Finance Transformation team leads innovation to combine data-driven finance with the AI approach driving accuracy, next gen forecasting capabilities, speed, efficiency, and reliability by exploring new techniques in ML and AI and building full stack services in AWS. This Agent-first Finance builders team will lead Finance Application Architecture, designs for automating decks, predicting outcomes, automation financial workdlows, forecasting values with high degree of automation and ML Ops for existing engineering pipelines and frameworks.

As an software engineer, you will own components of an integrated system. You will design and develop these components using AI builder tools, AWS and serverless infrastructure in the cloud that will need to be deployed for use by our financial stakeholders. You will work on a secured data management service that will allow the storage and usage of financial data with the highest standard of privacy. You will engage with a group of senior developers to deliver front end and backend solutions with GenAI-assisted software development builder tools and workflows to generate & optimize code, build tests, serve APIs, automate ML and Stats training and development pipelines and build orchestrations to serve with continuous evaluation and model training.

Key job responsibilities
You will utilize GenAI-assisted software development on a daily basis that integrates with artificial intelligence tools like Amazon Q Developer into builder workflows to generate & optimize code, build tests, explain unfamiliar code, and learn new languages or APIs, effectively boosting your team’s productivity and code quality. Throughout the software development lifecycle, you will deploy AI tools, agents, use Model Context Protocol (MCP) and large language models (LLMs) to assist in multiple phases - from requirements analysis to coding and testing.

We are looking for individuals who thrive in a collaborative environment where they will have a high level of independence, autonomy, and ownership in what they deliver. The right candidate will wear many hats and work in a highly collaborative environment that is more startup than big company.

A day in the life
As a Software Development Engineer, you will work with a team of talented engineers to build low-latency solutions for frontend, middle tier and backend as well as identify and evaluate new technology options for the automation challenges we are trying to solve. You will work with a variety of core languages, microservices, and technologies including Java, Javascript, Python, Dynamo DB, Lambda, SQS, SNS and many other AWS services. We are looking for a smart engineer who can effectively deal with ambiguity and work independently to clarify requirements, build prototypes and deliver results quickly. Come join a team in which builders build software and delight customers! You will learn, have fun, and make a positive impact for our customers.

jobs.description:
Esta é uma posição de tempo integral, permanente, presencial baseada em ESA8 - Osasco, BR e não oferece benefícios de realocação. Os candidatos devem estar localizados e legalmente autorizados a trabalhar em Brazil para serem elegíveis para consideração.

Quer crescer profissionalmente, se desafiar e fazer história? Na Amazon, isso é possível. Nossas equipes de logística transformam, todos os dias, a maneira como nos conectamos com clientes ao redor do mundo, resolvendo grandes desafios da indústria.

No time de transporte da Amazon, garantimos que cada pacote – grande ou pequeno – chegue ao seu destino, cumprindo nossa promessa aos clientes.

A equipe de UTR First Mile é responsável por garantir a excelência operacional nas etapas iniciais da cadeia logística da Amazon no Brasil. Atuamos na gestão e supervisão de dezenas de nodes de First Mile, colaborando com equipes multifuncionais como Middle Mile, Configuration, Capacity Planning, Supply Team, Delivery Stations e parceiros EDSP para assegurar que os pacotes sejam coletados, processados e despachados com máxima eficiência e qualidade.

Nesta posição, será responsável por monitorar, analisar e melhorar continuamente os processos operacionais de qualidade nos nodes de First Mile, garantindo a conformidade com os padrões da Amazon e impulsionando a experiência do cliente,

Se você busca desafios, crescimento e um ambiente que valoriza a inovação, essa pode ser a oportunidade certa para você!

A Amazon está comprometida com um ambiente de trabalho diverso e inclusivo. A Amazon é um empregador que oferece igualdade de oportunidades e não discrimina com base em raça, origem nacional, gênero, identidade de gênero, orientação sexual, status de veterano protegido, deficiência, idade ou outro status legalmente protegido.

Key job responsibilities
● Estar presencialmente na estação de coleta. Amazon lidera operações 24-7, e portanto, trabalhar em diferentes turnos será parte dessa função, podendo incluir finais de semana e feriados.
● Monitorar e analisar indicadores de qualidade operacional (KPIs) dos nodes de First Mile, incluindo taxas de falha de scan, wrong node, stow success rate e depart success rate
● Realizar auditorias de qualidade nos processos de recebimento, stow, depart e integração de pacotes nos nodes
● Identificar causas raiz de desvios de qualidade e propor planos de ação corretivos e preventivos
● Acompanhar métricas de desempenho por operational flow (AMZL E2E e TBAV), identificando nodes ofensores e oportunidades de melhoria
● Colaborar com equipes operacionais, parceiros EDSP e stakeholders internos para implementar melhorias de processo
● Desenvolver e manter dashboards e relatórios de qualidade para suportar a tomada de decisão
● Participar de projetos de melhoria contínua e automação de processos, atraves do uso de IA
● Garantir a padronização de processos de qualidade em todos os nodes da rede First Mile
● Suportar o onboarding e treinamento de novos membros da equipe em processos e padrões de qualidade
● Viajar 40-50% do tempo para apoiar lançamentos no local e o envolvimento dos parceiros.
